Craft with armor to teleport away and heal when dying
message.hp_end_expansion.ender_snail_she: %s Ender Snail Shell layers saved you from death
message.hp_end_expansion.end_ship_compas: The compass tears open a rift to the End Ship destination
message.hp_end_expansion.ender_core_boun: Ender Core bound to current position
message.hp_end_expansion.ender_core_sent: Target sent to the Ender Core bound position
patchouli.hp_end_expansion.end_expansion: Records creature appearances, names, mechanics, and spawning rules in HP End Expansion.$(br2)This guide will keep growing as new content is added.
patchouli.hp_end_expansion.category.crea: Creatures, pets, and ecological helpers added by HP End Expansion.
patchouli.hp_end_expansion.entry.overvie: $(entity)Void Whales$() are giant rideable creatures; $(entity)Ender Boxes$() are portable storage pets; $(entity)Ender Snails$() manage chorus plants; $(entity)Ender Fish$() drift through the void and leave unstable rifts.$(br2)These creatures focus on End exploration, storage, chorus automation, and ship-route teleportation.
$(mechanic)Pet Bags$() can capture tameable pets with right-click, making relocation and sorting easier. Right-click a block with a filled Pet Bag to release the stored creature again.
patchouli.hp_end_expansion.entry.void_wh: $(entity)Void Whales$() are giant void creatures that roam high above the End. Their huge bodies are easy to spot from far away.

$(mechanic)Taming: $()Interact with an untamed whale using Ender Pearls. After enough progress, it becomes tame.$(br)$(mechanic)Riding: $()The owner can right-click to ride. Movement input controls direction, and sneak descends.

Holding an Ender Pearl while riding grants extra movement speed. Each second, the Void Whale has a small chance to eat one pearl from the rider's hand.$(br2)The rider can spend Ender Pearls to perform a long horizontal teleport, mainly changing the XZ position.
$(spawn)Spawn: $()Void Whales naturally spawn in End biome tags with low weight, usually one at a time.$(br2)Tamed Void Whales try to stay within 48 blocks of their owner.
patchouli.hp_end_expansion.entry.ender_b: $(entity)Ender Boxes$() are walking End storage pets, shaped like living containers.
$(mechanic)Taming: $()Feed with Chorus Fruit. Once tamed, it follows the player and receives one initial End Ship style loot fill.$(br2)Right-click a tamed Ender Box to open its 54-slot container.
The owner can sneak-right-click an Ender Box to pack it and its contents into an item. Right-click a block with the item to release the entity again.
$(spawn)Spawn: $()Ender Boxes are not ordinary wild spawns. They are mainly obtained through spawn eggs, item release, or future structure content.
patchouli.hp_end_expansion.entry.ender_s: $(entity)Ender Snails$() are chorus gardeners. Chorus crystal clusters slowly grow on their shells.
Ender Snails move toward mature chorus plants and eat the whole plant. Untamed snails clean plants and leave a new flower behind; tamed snails drop the harvest or store it in nearby containers.
$(mechanic)Taming: $()Chorus Fruit can tame Ender Snails.$(br2)Tamed snails search for nearby immature Chorus Flowers, rub against them, and greatly speed up chorus tree growth with End particles.

New Ender Snails are usually babies. When a baby grows its tenth chorus cluster, it grows up, drops an Ender Snail Shell, and becomes an adult.$(br2)Adult tamed snails with the same owner can breed using Chorus Flowers.

Shells no longer come from killing snails. They come from baby molting.$(br2)A shell can be crafted with armor to provide one death-prevention layer: it teleports you to a nearby safe spot and heals based on shell layers.
$(spawn)Spawn: $()Ender Snails naturally spawn in End biome tags, with a higher weight than Void Whales. They usually appear in groups of 1 to 2.
patchouli.hp_end_expansion.entry.ender_f: $(entity)Ender Fish$() are small rift-shaped void fish. Their square lantern head, vertical fins, split ring tail, and uneven glowing tendrils make them distinct from Void Whales.
$(spawn)Spawn: $()Ender Fish appear in the End void, usually in loose groups of 2 to 3.$(br2)While swimming, they have a small chance to phase a short distance, leaving portal particles behind.

Killing an Ender Fish creates an $(mechanic)Ender Rift$() at its death position. Touching the rift sends you to the nearest configured End Ship destination.$(br2)Ender Fish also have a 20% chance to drop an Ender Core Shard.
Craft an End Ship Compass from a compass and an Ender Core Shard. Right-clicking it tears open a route toward the nearest configured End Ship destination.

Nine Ender Core Shards craft one Ender Core.$(br2)Sneak-right-click to bind the current position. Attacking a non-player creature with the bound core in your main hand sends that target to the bound position.
